reasoning: Honduras has experienced a steep decline in violence and homicides under a long-running 'state of exception' (e.g., the homicide rate nearly halved between 2022 and 2024, and continued falling from 25.3 to 23.2 in 2025). Because the reference value (34.917) is a trailing 12-month average in a strongly downward-trending environment, a given 30-day forward period in early to mid-2026 is mathematically very likely to fall below this past average. Additionally, the recent 2025 general elections likely moderately elevated the 360-day historical average with political violence; post-election periods typically experience a lull. Consequently, I assign a low probability (30-36%) that early 2026 30-day periods will register 35 or more events. For later dates, uncertainty increases: the 'state of exception' may lose effectiveness and Honduras has a history of extremely high gang violence, so mean reversion dictates that the probabilities approach 0.50 over longer horizons, especially during the next election cycle in 2029.
